title: Median income or consumption per day vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Median income or consumption per day vs. GDP per capita

This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This scatter plot compares median income or consumption per day against GDP per capita for various countries in 2024, with both axes plotted on a logarithmic scale. The vertical axis represents median income or consumption per day in international dollars at 2021 prices, ranging from $2 to over $50. The horizontal axis displays GDP per capita in the same currency and price basis, spanning from $2,000 to $50,000. Data points are color-coded by region: North America in orange, South America in brown, Africa in purple, Europe in blue, Asia in teal, and Oceania in light blue. Circle sizes correspond to population, with a reference scale indicating sizes for 1.4 billion and 600 million people. The United States sits in the top right corner, showing the highest values on both axes, well above $50 per day and $50,000 GDP per capita. Japan and several European nations cluster in the upper right quadrant with high GDP and median daily amounts between $20 and $50. China, represented by a large teal circle, sits near the center with a GDP per capita around $20,000 and a median daily value slightly above $10. India and Indonesia appear as large circles in the lower-middle section, with GDP per capita between $5,000 and $10,000 and median daily values around $5 to $10. African nations such as Burundi, DR Congo, Madagascar, and Uganda occupy the bottom left, with GDP per capita below $5,000 and median daily values between $2 and $5. A general positive trend is visible, where higher GDP per capita correlates with higher median income or consumption per day, though the slope and spread vary across regions.
